relevantLegalEntityAndOtherRegistrablePe.title: What are RLEs and ORPs?
relevantLegalEntityAndOtherRegistrablePe.line1: A relevant legal entity (RLE) is a corporate body or firm that can legally act as a PSC.
relevantLegalEntityAndOtherRegistrablePe.line2: An other registrable person (ORP) is a non-corporate entity or office that can legally act as a PSC. This can be:
relevantLegalEntityAndOtherRegistrablePe.bullet1: a corporation sole - a single incorporated office occupied by one person, such as Lord Mayor of London
relevantLegalEntityAndOtherRegistrablePe.bullet2: a government or government department of a country, a territory, or a part of a country or territory
relevantLegalEntityAndOtherRegistrablePe.bullet3: an international organisation whose members include two or more countries or territories (or their governments)
relevantLegalEntityAndOtherRegistrablePe.bullet4: a local authority or local government body in the UK or elsewhere

tellUsAboutPscPage.title: Tell us about people with significant control (PSCs)
tellUsAboutPscPage.whatWeAsk: We'll ask you if the partnership will have any PSCs on registration. If it will, you need to tell us about them.
tellUsAboutPscPage.addingMoreAfter: After the partnership is registered, you'll need to file a paper form if you need to add any more PSCs.
tellUsAboutPscPage.psc: A person with significant control (PSC) is someone who owns or controls the partnership. They do this by meeting one or more conditions known as 'natures of control'.
tellUsAboutPscPage.votingRights: more than 25% ownership of voting rights
tellUsAboutPscPage.rights: the right to appoint or remove management
tellUsAboutPscPage.influence: significant influence or control over the partnership
tellUsAboutPscPage.firmOrTrust: They can also be a PSC if they have significant control over a firm or trust that meets any of these natures of control.
tellUsAboutPscPage.pscType: A PSC can be an individual person, a relevant legal entity (RLE) or an other registrable person (ORP).
tellUsAboutPscPage.guidance: read the guidance on PSCs of Scottish limited partnerships (opens in new tab).

willThePartnershipHaveAnyPscPage.title: Will the partnership have any PSCs on registration?
willThePartnershipHaveAnyPscPage.optionYes: Yes, at least one person will count as a PSC in relation to the partnership. This may be an individual, a relevant legal entity (RLE) or an other registrable person (ORP)
willThePartnershipHaveAnyPscPage.optionNo: No, there is no person identified as a PSC in relation to the limited partnership
willThePartnershipHaveAnyPscPage.errorMessage: Select if the partnership will have any PSCs on registration
willThePartnershipHaveAnyPscPage.publicRegisterLine1: We'll show this information on the public register.

whichTypePage.title: Which type of person with significant control (PSC) do you need to add?
whichTypePage.errorMessage: Select which type of PSC you need to add

commonEntityFields.governingLaw: What governing law does it operate under?
commonEntityFields.legalFormOrCorporateIdentity: What is its legal form or corporate identity?
commonEntityFields.legalFormOrCorporateIdentityHint: This is the corporate body type. For example, private limited company.
commonEntityFields.publicRegisterLine1: We'll show all of this information on the public register.

addRelevantLegalEntity.legalEntityName: What is the relevant legal entity (RLE)'s name?

addRelevantLegalEntity.whichRegisterHint: For example, the Register of Companies UK.
addRelevantLegalEntity.countryRegistered: Which country is it registered in? (if applicable)
addRelevantLegalEntity.registrationNumber: What is the registration number? (if applicable)

addOtherRegistrablePerson.title: Add an other registrable person PSC
addOtherRegistrablePerson.legalEntityName: What is the other registrable person (ORP)'s name?

addIndividualPerson.title: Add a PSC (individual person)
addIndividualPerson.consent: This person knows their details are being supplied as part of this application
addIndividualPerson.secondNationality: What is their second nationality (if relevant)?
addIndividualPerson.secondNationalityHint: Dual citizenship (also known as dual nationality) is allowed in some countries. This means you can be a citizen of your own country and another country.
addIndividualPerson.publicRegisterLine1: We'll show the month and year of the PSC's date of birth, but not the exact date.
addIndividualPerson.publicRegisterLine2: We'll show all the other information you provide on this screen on the public register.

whichTypeOfNatureOfControlPage.publicRegisterInformationLine1: We'll show this information on the public register.

relevantLegalEntity.title: How does this RLE hold control over the limited partnership?

individual.hint: The RLE meets one or more natures of control

firm.hint: The RLE has control over a firm that meets one or more natures of control

trust.hint: The RLE has control over a trust that meets one or more natures of control

otherRegistrablePerson.title: How does this ORP hold control over the limited partnership?

individual.hint: The ORP meets one or more natures of control

firm.hint: The ORP has control over a firm that meets one or more natures of control

trust.hint: The ORP has control over a trust that meets one or more natures of control

individualPerson.title: How does this individual hold control over the limited partnership?

individual.hint: The individual meets one or more natures of control

firm.hint: The individual has control over a firm that meets one or more natures of control

trust.hint: The individual has control over a trust that meets one or more natures of control

addNatureOfControlPage.publicRegisterInformationLine1: We'll show this information on the public register.
addNatureOfControlPage.moreThan25LessThan50: more than 25% but not more than 50%
addNatureOfControlPage.moreThan50LessThan75: more than 50% but not more than 75%
addNatureOfControlPage.ownershipOfRightToAppointOrRemove: Ownership of right to appoint or remove management
addNatureOfControlPage.selectSigInfluenceHint: Only select this statement if the other natures of control do not apply

individual.title: What is the individual's nature of control?
individual.titleHint: You've told us that the individual meets one or more natures of control.
individual.shareOfAssetsHint: The individual holds (directly or indirectly) the right to the following percentage of surplus assets of the partnership on a winding up:
individual.votingRightsHint: The individual holds (directly or indirectly) the following percentage of voting rights in the limited partnership:
individual.ownershipCheckboxText: The individual holds, directly or indirectly, the right to appoint or remove a majority of the persons entitled to take part in the management of the limited partnership.
individual.sigInfluenceCheckboxText: The individual has the right to exercise, or actually exercises, significant influence or control over the limited partnership.

firm.title: What is the firm's nature of control?
firm.titleHint: You've told us that the individual has control over a firm that meets one or more natures of control.
firm.shareOfAssetsHint: The members of the firm hold (directly or indirectly) the right to the following percentage of surplus assets of the partnership on a winding up:
firm.votingRightsHint: The members of the firm hold (directly or indirectly) the following percentage of voting rights in the limited partnership:
firm.ownershipCheckboxText: The members of the firm hold, directly or indirectly, the right to appoint or remove a majority of the persons entitled to take part in the management of the limited partnership.
firm.sigInfluenceCheckboxText: The members of the firm have the right to exercise, or actually exercises, significant influence or control over the limited partnership.

trust.title: What is the trust's nature of control?
trust.titleHint: You've told us that the individual has control over a trust that meets one or more natures of control.
trust.shareOfAssetsHint: The trustees of the trust hold (directly or indirectly) the right to the following percentage of surplus assets of the partnership on a winding up:
trust.votingRightsHint: The trustees of the trust hold (directly or indirectly) the following percentage of voting rights in the limited partnership:
trust.ownershipCheckboxText: The trustees of the trust hold, directly or indirectly, the right to appoint or remove a majority of the persons entitled to take part in the management of the limited partnership.
trust.sigInfluenceCheckboxText: The trustees of the trust have the right to exercise, or actually exercises, significant influence or control over the limited partnership.

removePscPage.errorMessage: Select yes if you need to remove a PSC
